explicit_text: I served in the United States Army infantry during World War II, specifically in the 84th Infantry Division. My service spanned three years in the European Theater of Operations, where I experienced some of the most intense battles of the war. The Army was my branch, and it was through it that I participated in campaigns across France, Germany, and beyond.
implicit_text: I served in the infantry branch of the U.S. military during World War II, specifically in a division known for its role in the European campaigns. My service spanned three years in the European Theater of Operations, where I experienced some of the most intense battles of the war. It was through this military branch that I participated in campaigns across France, Germany, and beyond.

explicit_text: I was proud to be part of the 84th Infantry Division, which saw action in several major campaigns during World War II. We landed in Normandy in September and pushed through France, Paris, Holland, and into Germany, fighting in the Rhineland and Central Europe campaigns. The division was also heavily involved in the Battle of the Bulge and later held positions near the Elbe River outside Berlin.

entity: European Theater of World War II
explicit_text: I served three years in the European Theater of World War II, where I fought in some of the war's most critical battles. From landing in Normandy to pushing through France and Germany, this theater was the center of my military experience. The harsh winter conditions during the Battle of the Bulge and the final push to the Elbe River were defining moments of my service.

From landing in Normandy to pushing through France and Germany, this theater was the center of my military experience. The harsh winter conditions during the Battle of the Bulge and the final push to the Elbe River were defining moments of my service.

explicit_text: The Battle of the Bulge was my most memorable wartime experience. We were pretty well isolated for a long time due to bad weather, which prevented us from getting support. Surviving that ordeal and eventually being relieved around Christmas time in late 1944 was something I will never forget.

This campaign was crucial as we made our way to the Elbe River outside Berlin.

explicit_text: I fought in the Rhineland Campaign where we made significant advances into Germany. After landing in Normandy and moving through France and Holland, we established a rallying point before attacking the Siegfried Line. explicit_text: We reached the Elbe River outside of Berlin near the end of the war in Europe. Our division probably could have entered Berlin, but we were held at the Elbe as the Russians took the city. It was memorable sitting on the western shore and seeing thousands of prisoners surrender to us rather than the Soviets.

explicit_text: We were close to entering Berlin at the end of the war but were held back at the Elbe River to let the Russians take the city. I believe we could have gotten there a week before the Soviets. Berlin was a major objective, but history dictated the division of control between the Allies and the Soviet Union.

explicit_text: I visited the World War II Memorial about two weeks ago and was very impressed by it. Although I felt there was little there that personally related to my service, the Memorial was well done and tasteful. I appreciated how it respected the view of the Mall and thought it was a credit to those who built it.
implicit_text: I visited the national memorial dedicated to the global conflict about two weeks ago and was very impressed by it. Although I felt there was little there that personally related to my service, the Memorial was well done and tasteful. I appreciated how it respected the view of the central Washington, D.C. park and thought it was a credit to those who built it.

explicit_text: I noticed that the World War II Memorial had posts for states and territories but lacked specific references to battles like Iwo Jima. I thought it would have been better if GIs, sailors, or Marines could see battles such as Iwo Jima represented so they could relate more personally. It’s a significant battle, but it wasn’t highlighted in a way that connected to veterans like me.

explicit_text: I was initially concerned that the World War II Memorial might disrupt the view of The Mall in Washington, D.C., but after seeing it, I think they did a marvelous job. The Memorial fits well within the space and honors the veterans in a respectful way. Visiting The Mall and the Memorial was a meaningful experience for me and my son.

explicit_text: On the way home from Europe, we traveled on a flat-bottom Puerto Rican banana boat across the Atlantic in January. The boat had no keel and tossed us around in the storms, making the trip very rough. I spent about eleven of the fourteen days in my bunk, but we finally made it back to New York safely.
implicit_text: On the way home from Europe, we traveled on a flat-bottom banana boat from a Caribbean island across the Atlantic in January. The boat had no keel and tossed us around in the storms, making the trip very rough. I spent about eleven of the fourteen days in my bunk, but we finally made it back to New York safely.

explicit_text: After our long and rough Atlantic crossing on a Puerto Rican banana boat, we arrived back in New York City. It was a relief to be home after years of service in Europe. New York was the port where our journey ended and where I was finally able to rest after the war.
